items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

articles.title: 'More bad news' expected as testing widens after second WA bird flu case

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

image.altText: A man with beanie on holding a chicken with chickens on ground

articles.title: Poultry producers warn bird flu would be 'catastrophic for Tasmania'

children.content: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

image.altText: Brad stands in front of his farm gate, which has a biosecurity sign on it. He crosses his arms and looks at the camera.

articles.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

image.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

gallery.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A front-on shot of a white van driving at night with headlights on and a police van to the right.

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The controversial herbicide paraquat — previously linked to Parkinson's disease — can continue to be used safely with "significant" restrictions, including lower application rates, Australia's chemical regulator says.

gallery.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

thumbnail.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: Alessandro Circati chose Australia over Italy. His dad approves

image.altText: Alessandro Circati celebrates after Socceroos' win over Türkiye at FIFA World Cup.

children.content: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

items.title: Fourth WA council in turmoil after three councillors quit in three days

children.content: Three councillors have quit the City of South Perth in three days, citing concerns over a rates hike and broader dysfunction within the council.

A photo taken of a streetscape from a high-rise
A board showing the councillors in the City of South Perth

items.title: 'No longer appropriate': WA MP calls to end Lord's Prayer in parliament

children.content: A Labor MP says he wants the Lord’s Prayer scrapped from WA parliament, despite the reading of the Christian prayer being a long-held tradition before sessions.

image.altText: Premier Peter Malinauskas 2025-09-19 09:09:00

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports — as it happened

children.content: WiseTech shares finished the day lower by 3.3 per cent after Monday's 18 per cent dive, as executive chair Richard White "emphatically and unequivocally" has denied human trafficking reports. The ASX 200 closed 0.3 per cent lower.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.

image.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

items.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

items.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

items.title: What we know about how H5N1 bird flu got here and where it may appear next

children.content: Authorities were worried about avian influenza coming in from the north, until the virus started moving across the Southern Ocean. Now infected birds have been found in Western Australia. Where else could birds carrying the virus land?

articles.title: Second Australian case of deadly bird flu confirmed

children.content: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

image.altText: Giant Northern Petrel

items.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

image.altText: Hugh Jackman in The Death of Robin Hood

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

image.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

items.title: Meet the 'King of the North' set to become the UK's new PM

children.content: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

image.altText: A man poses for a selfie in front of a large crowd.

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

image.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

image.altText: the earth rotates and is surrounded by tiny reflective particles

items.title: Scientists 'blown away' by baby coral resilience on Ningaloo Reef

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

gallery.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.
Spawn cones deployed in the Bundegi Reef to collect baby corals in a bid to restore bleached corals in Ningaloo Reef.
Marine researchers and scientists from various organisations working at the Mesa Reef in Exmouth to restore the bleched corals

thumbnail.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.
Spawn cones deployed in the Bundegi Reef to collect baby corals in a bid to restore bleached corals in Ningaloo Reef.
Marine researchers and scientists from various organisations working at the Mesa Reef in Exmouth to restore the bleched corals
